google_oauth2_protocol.rule: OAuth 2.0 Flow: Mandatory use of 'Authorization Code Flow' with PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange) for the confidential client.
Scope Restriction: Strictly limit scopes to 'https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.send'. Do not request full account access.
Token Management: Implement 'offline access' to obtain Refresh Tokens. Refresh Tokens must be stored securely (encrypted at rest if persisted).
Token Relaying: If the microservice acts as a proxy, ensure the original user context is preserved or use a service account with delegated authority where applicable.

spring_security_configuration.rule: Stateful vs Stateless: The API must be Stateless (SessionCreationPolicy.STATELESS) using JWT for inbound authentication.
CSRF & CORS: Disable CSRF for the API layer (since it is stateless). Configure a strict CORS policy allowing only authorized internal domains.
Sensitive Data: Prohibit hardcoding Client Secrets or API Keys. Use Spring 'Environment' or 'Vault' integration.

api_and_swagger_protection.rule: Public Endpoints: Only /swagger-ui.html, /v3/api-docs/**, and /actuator/health/** are permitted for unauthenticated access.
Business Endpoints: All /api/** paths must require a valid Bearer Token with specific 'SCOPE_mail.write' authority.

implementation_details.rule: Server-to-Server Auth: Use Native Google SDK ('GoogleCredentials') with injected Refresh Tokens for automated token lifecycle management in Headless/CLI modes, strictly bypassing Spring Security OAuth2 Client session dependencies.
